entries.title: Evaporation
entries.definition: Heat from the sun turns liquid water into invisible water vapour.

entries.title: Transpiration
entries.definition: Plants release water vapour into the air through their leaves.

entries.title: Condensation
entries.definition: Rising water vapour cools and turns into droplets, forming clouds.

entries.title: Precipitation
entries.definition: Water falls from clouds as rain, snow or hail.

entries.title: Infiltration
entries.definition: Water soaks down into the soil and rock, becoming groundwater.

entries.title: Surface Runoff
entries.definition: Water that flows over the land into streams and rivers.

entries.title: The Water Cycle
entries.definition: The continuous movement of water: evaporation, condensation, precipitation and runoff.

entries.title: Uneven Distribution
entries.definition: Water is spread unevenly, so some places have plenty and others face shortages.

entries.title: Water Contamination
entries.definition: Pollutants make water unsafe for people and ecosystems.

entries.title: Floods
entries.definition: Too much water arriving too fast to drain away, often worsened by sealed surfaces.

entries.title: Drought
entries.definition: A long dry period with little rainfall, causing water shortages.

entries.title: Water Management
entries.definition: Sustainable ways to supply water: harvesting, recycling and using it efficiently.

entries.title: NEWater
entries.definition: Highly treated, recycled used water — one of Singapore's Four National Taps.

entries.title: Forest Layers
entries.definition: The four rainforest layers: emergent, canopy, understorey and forest floor.

entries.title: Canopy
entries.definition: The dense upper roof of treetops that catches most of the sunlight.

entries.title: Equatorial Climate
entries.definition: Hot and wet all year round, near the Equator.

entries.title: Drip-Tip Leaves
entries.definition: Pointed leaf tips that shed heavy rain quickly to prevent rot.

entries.title: Buttress Roots
entries.definition: Wide roots that support tall trees in thin rainforest soil.

entries.title: Adaptations
entries.definition: Special plant features that suit the hot, wet climate.

entries.title: Biodiversity
entries.definition: The huge variety of plant and animal life in an ecosystem.

entries.title: Deforestation
entries.definition: Clearing forest for farming, logging and building.

entries.title: Effects of Deforestation
entries.definition: Lost biodiversity, soil erosion and destroyed habitats.

entries.title: Conservation
entries.definition: Using nature sustainably so it stays healthy for the future.

entries.title: Mangrove Coast
entries.definition: A salty, muddy, tidal coastal environment most plants can't survive.

entries.title: Prop Roots
entries.definition: Stilt roots that anchor mangroves in soft mud against the tide.

entries.title: Breathing Roots
entries.definition: Roots that grow upward out of the mud to take in oxygen.

entries.title: Value of Mangroves
entries.definition: Coast protection, wildlife nurseries and carbon storage.

entries.title: Coastal Protection
entries.definition: Mangrove roots absorb wave energy, shielding the shore.

entries.title: Threats to Mangroves
entries.definition: Clearing for development and aquaculture destroys mangroves.
